text.title: LLM Fine-tuning (General Guide) — Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of `finetuning` must enforce. Verifiable at review time; failures gate the artifact.

rule.statement: Score prompt+RAG baselines on the held-out eval set BEFORE committing to fine-tuning.

rationale.source: Anthropic prompt-engineering guidance; OpenAI fine-tuning docs

rule.rationale: Fine-tuning is least reversible; prompt+RAG often closes 60-80% of the quality gap at 1% of the cost.

rule.statement: Require ≥100 examples for LoRA/QLoRA, ≥1000 for full FT, ≥10000 for safety/alignment SFT.

rationale.source: Hu et al. 2021 LoRA; OpenAI best practices

rule.rationale: Below floors the fine-tune overfits and degrades on held-out examples.

rule.statement: Block deployment until the fine-tune beats baseline on held-out eval on ≥2 task-relevant metrics.
rule.rationale: Without an eval gate, fine-tunes ship that look better on training data and worse on production traffic.

rule.statement: API SFT ≤10k examples; QLoRA for 7-13B + single A100; LoRA for 7-70B + multi-GPU; Full FT only when ≥100k + 8×A100.

rationale.source: Dettmers et al. 2023 QLoRA; HuggingFace PEFT

rule.rationale: Each technique has a known break-even on data/hardware; wrong tier wastes money or signal.

rule.statement: Mix 20-30% general-domain data with task data to prevent catastrophic forgetting.

rationale.source: Bai et al. 2022; Anthropic constitutional AI

rule.rationale: Pure-task fine-tunes lose general capabilities and degrade off-distribution.

rule.statement: Every fine-tune ships with a decision record listing technique, data, framework, baseline + eval-gate scores, rollback owner.
rule.rationale: Without an ADR the org cannot reproduce the choice or roll back.
